Not investment advice. 

Seek a duly licensed professional for investment advice##

tweet.text:
April 28, 2026: My Actions & Thoughts on OpenAI Sell-Off (Another Rant?)
$MU $SNDK $LITE $MRVL $GEV $CLS $CIEN $NBIS $BE $AAOI $Q $TXN $ONDS

Top Up: $MU $SNDK $CLS
New: $TXN 
Deleted: $COHR $IPGP $TSEM $MOD $CCJ

A) The Art of Staying Calm in a Sea of Red

A huge red PnL day is perfectly normal when you have an AI-concentrated portfolio like mine. It is delusional to think that AI stocks (& hence my PnL) can only go up everyday. Adjust your unrealistic expectations if you haven't.

This is definitely not the first time, and also not the last time of occurence of a red PnL, and what happened in the past three years is that my PnL continues to hit new ATHs after that. 2024 March's AI fear, 2025 Deepseek, Tariff, Unwinding of the Carry Trade, Michael Burry & AI Capex Fear,........Will this time be different, what's the context this time?

The key is maintain your calm and remember why you do this for. Are you a day trader/ shorter swing trader or a longer term investor? Completely different actions can be all right according to your personal circumstances, if you know what you are doing.

For me, it is to check if there is any change in the thesis, and if the uptrend is still intact (for my longer timeframe most of the cases is a yes, a single bearish day doesn't reverse the trend), is the support still holding (NEVER sell at a support in an uptrend). 

Note there are some exceptions when I deal with smaller positions or recent buys though, as my consideration is done on a whole portfolio basis instead of being obsessed with doing it perfect on minor individual stock level. I never go for perfect or a vanilla treatment, but at my subjective but emotionless discretion.

B) Open AI News

So the end of last year we had this AI Capex fear, which is supposed to be a positive thing for a certain group when hyperscalers increase their spending, and now we have this fear that Open AI may not be able to spend as much.

I won't read too much into it yet, when all the other hyperscalers, $META $GOOGL $AMZN $MSFT are going to report their earnings in these couple of days, and we will have better clarity then on their revenue and their continous commitment on AI capex.

C) Actions intraday (mix)

I am not a one day bullish one day bearish guy, or a sell everything today and buy everything tomorrow guy. My actions in the day can be mixed, depending on how I see them on their own merits.

1. Top Up

Significant top up made into $MU (position raised to 40%ish right after I partially trimmed it a few days ago), as I think it has proven itself that technically it can conquered the $500 level, and when I look around, it is hard to find another stock with such a level of visibility, certainty and valuation. Why stick my neck into some new hot themes when I can simply increase my exposure in an existing leader?

$SNDK - just a minor top up ahead of its earnings on 30/4. My profit cushion is still huge, over 100%, on just the existing holding (not counting profit takings/ additional swings along the way).

$CLS reported earnings, and I top up into it by setting $350 as a hard stop, just $0.01 below the LoD. Normally I don't like round number, would prefer to set e.g. $345 or $349.90, but in this case I guess I wasn't too obsessed of keeping this name. In this case, the stop wasn't triggered today, but it should be kept at $350, would want to see this name start working else I am fine with letting it go.

2. $TXN

One of the better earnings gap up / BO. 

A close to 20% earnings move, followed by three day of lower volume digestion into the candle body & 5sma, hence it triggered my first buy at 3%.

Note there is no guarantee that it won't drop further below 5sma and take out the earnings gap up low (if there is such guarantee my size wouldn't be just 3%....), I will be bullish bias above the earnings gap which start from $236 (and where the 21ema catching up from below) and looking for chance to add (if the market condition and my capital allows).

3. Deletions

A bunch of these, mainly to provide the SoF for the top ups above. These are names which are relatively small/ new and hasn't seen much traction $COHR $IPGP $TSEM $MOD $CCJ. 

$IPGP started as a speculative play and treated as such. $CCJ was out at a gain, but its performance has been lagging, and I consider it to be not an efficient use of my leveraged capital. $MOD and $TSEM are new buys which failed immediately and I don't like starting positions with a loss. $COHR seems to fall right into a support (hence I shouldn't have sold it). 

But overall they were done more as a "less is more", focus shifting towards $MU. I still like their thesis, and will keep them on watch for potential re-entries, if the market and my capital allows.

D) Lastly

Remember this is just me.

You don't want to be me, be him. Aspire to be you.

profile_bio.description: A Trend Following Theme Investor with a learning-driven approach to trading. Process over Outcome, Context over Patterns, Stocks over Setups, EC over Ego.

quoted_tweet.text:
April 24, 2026 - Process, not PnL
$LITE $SNDK $MU $MRVL $CIEN $BE $GEV $CLS $AAOI $NBIS $Q $TSEM $TSLA $ONDS $IPGP $COHR $CCJ

A) Use of Leverage & Missing Opportunities

I did quite some deleveraging as the week closed with $MU hitting $500 and $SNDK hitting $1000. 

Being too heavy in overall portfolio (was 170% in) did tie up my mental flexibility to be more proactive and aggressive in new ideas e.g. those "CPU theme" of $AMD $ARM $INTC) and what else. 

But I also realise that I can't have my cake and eat it too, I probably have chosen to go bigger in names which I am more comfortable with instead of opening smaller starters in new names, and the end result might probably the same if not better.

But for sure this CPU theme will be on my watchlist.

B) Positions/ Theme at a glance

Strategy is mainly AI theme, where bottlenecks are at.

3 primary positions $LITE, $SNDK, $MU take up more than 50% weight, appropriately trimmed some before their coming earnings where applicable. $LITE is AI optical while $SNDK and $MU are AI memory.

The rest are still AI optical heavy, in line with my belief/ chart. $MRVL & $CIEN  take up another 16%, and $AAOI $TSEM $COHR $IPGP another 16%.

AI power $BE $GEV $CCJ 16%.

AI compute/ Semi related $NBIS $CLS $Q another 16%.

New starters, 3% each, which haven't gone anywhere
$ONDS, $TSLA.

(all % are approximate, I don't do exact).

C) Going forward

This current generation is probably the least patient of all, chasing for constant actions and instant gratifications with much shorter timeframes, as though sitting still through a pullback or simply let profits run is a sin.

Personally content with doing nothing, yet flexible enough to do anything as things develop.
